channel.title: OpenAI Research
channel.description: Research news and publications from OpenAI.

item.title: Inside our approach to the Model Spec
item.description: Learn how OpenAI’s Model Spec serves as a public framework for model behavior, balancing safety, user freedom, and accountability as AI systems advance.

item.title: How we monitor internal coding agents for misalignment
item.description: How OpenAI uses chain-of-thought monitoring to study misalignment in internal coding agents—analyzing real-world deployments to detect risks and strengthen AI safety safeguards.

item.title: Improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs
item.description: IH-Challenge trains models to prioritize trusted instructions, improving instruction hierarchy, safety steerability, and resistance to prompt injection attacks.

item.title: Reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought, and that’s good
item.description: OpenAI introduces CoT-Control and finds reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought, reinforcing monitorability as an AI safety safeguard.

item.title: Introducing GPT-5.4
item.description: Introducing GPT-5.4, OpenAI’s most most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work, with state-of-the-art coding, computer use, tool search, and 1M-token context.

item.title: Extending single-minus amplitudes to gravitons
item.description: A new preprint extends single-minus amplitudes to gravitons, with GPT-5.2 Pro helping derive and verify nonzero graviton tree amplitudes in quantum gravity.

item.title: GPT-5.3 Instant: Smoother, more useful everyday conversations
item.description: GPT-5.3 Instant: Smoother, more useful everyday conversations
